The Gesneriaceae family is part of the larger order Lamiales, which includes plants like mint, lavender, and snapdragons. Diastema gloxiniflorum, along with other members of the Gesneriaceae family, are primarily native to the Americas, with some species found in Africa. The classification of Diastema gloxiniflorum is further divided into its binomial nomenclature, which is used to identify specific species.

intro: Diastema gloxiniflorum, also known as "Tongue-leaf plant," is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Diastema gloxiniflorum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Diastema gloxiniflorum is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also used as a tincture or capsule supplement, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
